caribbean.headline: Moderate Winds and Elevated Seas Impact Eastern Caribbean Ports
caribbean.paragraph: St. Thomas is experiencing moderate easterly winds up to 16 knots with wave heights reaching 6.6 feet and a dominant swell period of 9 seconds from the ESE. Rain chances are elevated at 65 percent, while nearby St. Maarten reports slightly higher winds near 18 knots, indicating unsettled marine conditions across the region.

mediterranean.headline: Strong Winds and High Waves Challenge Mykonos Today
mediterranean.paragraph: Mykonos is experiencing sustained winds of 28 knots with waves reaching a significant height of 10.2 feet and a dominant west swell period of 9 seconds. Rain probability is very high at 98 percent, creating challenging conditions for mariners and port operations in the eastern Mediterranean.
